curation_notes: Re-curated with updated L3 hints. The patch modifies only src/waitress/channel.py (1 non-test source file). The fix adds a check in the received() method, under the requests_lock, to return False if will_close or close_when_flushed is set, preventing the race condition where a pipelined request could be queued and serviced after the connection was marked for closure due to an invalid first request. L3 area narrowed to channel-level data reception and pipelining queue management to distinguish from the broader L1 hint. L3 description adds recv_bytes boundary condition and TOCTOU framing. Vulnerability class race-condition confirmed from CWE-367 (TOCTOU) and the patch diff which adds lock-protected closure state checks.
